steps.title: Nimzowitsch's Idea
steps.text: Prophylaxis (from Nimzowitsch): make a move that PREVENTS what the opponent wants to do. Before your own plan, neutralize theirs. Karpov was the modern master — he would play "nothing" moves that quietly killed every hope.

steps.title: How to Spot It
steps.text: Before your move, ask: "If it were the opponent's turn, what would be their best move?" Then prevent it. A prophylactic move doesn't attack — it denies possibilities.
